Entry tranches: 50bp at ¥1,680 immediate, scale to 5% on pullbacks ¥1,500-1,600, aggressive add ≤¥1,500. NO CHASE >¥1,750. Funding: v10.2 retained +1pp dry powder + 1pp Tamron trim. TRIM: >¥2,200 no catalyst = trim 50%; 6mo Effissimo silence = re-eval.

Composite gate PASSES BOTH.

positions.notes: Silchester 5.05% disclosed Mar 2026, WAC ¥2,511. Tier 2 LONG-ONLY style (NOT Tier 1 activist) - thesis must stand on fundamentals + TSE reform. P/E 11.5x vs global peers 17-25x, 4.47% yield. India growth (Kansai Nerolac) hidden asset. Px below WAC = clean shadow co-investment. Entered Apr 1 2026 funded from Daito Trust trim. Patient 24-36mo horizon. [2026-04-30 VERIFICATION-AUDIT] Silchester (E24872) filed initial 5.05% on Apr 1, 2026 (stock +7% on news to ¥2,496). Filing language unusually activist for Silchester: explicit demands include 増配, 自己株式買い入れ, 金庫株消却, 資本政策の変更. Voting reservations on M&A, director appointments. Departs from Silchester's typical quiet-accumulator stylebook. Cross-reference Silchester's 65 Japan holdings: most are mature 8-19% multi-year ratchets (e.g., Sumitomo Osaka Cement 19.43%, Alfresa 12.11%, Suzuken 8.25%, Daito Trust 9.22%) — Kansai Paint is at the start of that pattern. Next catalyst: May 11 2026 earnings. No new filings/news past 7 days. [2026-05-01 NEWS-AUDIT] ?: 大量保有報告書ＤＢ [株主:E08957] 三井住友DS... (大量保有報告) [2026-05-16 NEWS-AUDIT] ?: 大量保有報告書ＤＢ [株主:E10677] アセットマネジメントOne > [8804]... (大量保有報告)
positions.thesis: Silchester long-only quality engagement. Japanese paint global #4 with significant India exposure (Kansai Nerolac). Trades at P/E 11.5x vs global paint peers (Sherwin ~25x, AkzoNobel ~17x), 4.47% yield. TSE PBR reform pressure + India growth = standalone case. Silchester pattern: patient 3-7yr holds, passive engagement, low-velocity.
positions.trigger: Watch: (1) Silchester accumulation past 7%+ (signals activist intent vs passive), (2) any cross-shareholder unwind announcement, (3) May 11 earnings, (4) India business strategic action (Kansai Nerolac listed separately)

last_filing.purpose: ACTIVIST INTENT (atypical for Silchester): may demand dividend hikes (増配), buyback frequency/total (自己株式の買い入れの頻度または総量), treasury share retirement (金庫株消却), other capital policy changes. May vote against M&A, asset disposals, director appointments. May add or sell.

positions.name: Suzuken

bear.rationale: Pharma wholesale margin compression / Silchester campaign stalls

base.rationale: New MTMP delivers modest dividend hike + buyback; PBR rerate to 0.85x

bull.rationale: Sector-wide capital return; Bank of Kyoto +42% analog; treasury stock cancellation

xbull.rationale: Cross-shareholding unwind + M&A speculation in pharma wholesale consolidation

positions.catalyst: May 2026 full-year results + new MTMP announcement
positions.thesis: Silchester at 8.25% — first time re-entering ABOVE prior 6.17% peak in their 17-year Japan history. Filing carries explicit capital-policy demand language (Bank of Kyoto template), NOT 純投資 boilerplate. Part of coordinated PHARMA WHOLESALE SECTOR campaign: Alfresa 12.11% + Medipal 11.71% + Suzuken 8.25%. Sub-1x PBR oligopoly, structurally over-capitalized, cross-shareholding heavy. FY24-FY26 MTMP expires Mar 2026, new MTMP at May 2026 full-year results = primary hard catalyst. Bank of Kyoto precedent: campaign produced +42% over 2-3 years; Silchester held through and beyond peak.
positions.trigger: PRIMARY: May 2026 full-year results + new MTMP announcement. SECONDARY (BUY-confirmation): Silchester 変更報告書 to 9-10%+ stake; Suzuken announces buyback/dividend hike/treasury stock cancellation pre-MTMP; Alfresa/Medipal escalation (sector confirmation). WATCH: Silchester reduction filing before MTMP would be primary bear signal.
positions.notes: Px ¥5,561 vs WAC ¥5,400 = +3.0% (close to NEUTRAL edge — clean shadow entry zone). Existing Alfresa 2% in book aligns with sector thesis - DON'T trim Alfresa to fund Suzuken. Multi-year compounding horizon (not binary AGM trade). Sector tailwind: 3-name coordinated capital return campaign is materially more probable than 3 separate trajectories. [2026-04-30 VERIFICATION-AUDIT] Silchester International Investors LLP at 8.25% (per Silchester Japan portfolio: Sumitomo Osaka Cement 19.43%, Alfresa 12.11%, Medipal 11.71%, Daito 9.22%, Suzuken 8.25%). LATE-CYCLE FLAG: Δ vs WAC +20.1% — past +15% co-investment gate. PWER 5.0% near WEAK_HOLD floor. Consider trim if Δ vs WAC crosses +25%. [2026-05-01 NEWS-AUDIT] ?: Ｍ＆Ａの世界へようこそ！ 大量保有報告制度について (大量保有報告) [2026-05-16 NEWS-AUDIT] ?: 2015年02月23日（月）に提出されたEDINET... (大量保有報告, 変更報告書)

last_filing.type: 変更報告書 (Re-accumulation past prior peak)

reasons: Silchester 8.25% — 34% ABOVE prior 6.17% peak. Highest conviction signal in their 17-year Japan history. Filing language explicit capital-policy demands (Bank of Kyoto template, NOT 純投資). Part of coordinated pharma wholesale sector campaign (Alfresa 12.11% + Medipal 11.71% + Suzuken 8.25%). Px +3.0% above WAC = near-neutral edge. Our PWER 24.7% > 20%. May 2026 MTMP hard catalyst. Multi-year compounding horizon → L3 patient compounder layer.

watch_list.name: Rengo
watch_list.status: MURAKAMI EARLY-STAGE — Stage 1-2 (silent→engagement), Change Report No. 1
watch_list.thesis: Murakami group early-stage entry confirmed. Aya Nomura 3.97% + ATRA 0.98% + C&I residual = ~5% aggregate. Lead filer 南青山不動産 (Aya Nomura's vehicle). Stage 1-2 in Murakami 5-step playbook. Filing age ~2.8 months, Change Report No. 1 = fresh entry, NOT late-stage redistribution. Px ¥1,275 BELOW activist WAC ¥1,340 = -4.9% (full co-investment edge intact). BUT: Rengo structural ROE 4.9% caps any campaign at ~0.85x PBR. No earnings/transformation inflection. PWER ~17% standalone — sub-20% threshold. L3 territory, not L1.
watch_list.trigger: PRIMARY (BUY trigger): (1) ATRA stake escalates >2% (matches Wavelock/Keikyu pattern signaling deeper deployment), (2) Change Report No. 2+ shows aggregate group rising 5%->8%, (3) MTMP capital return commitment from Rengo. SECONDARY (SELL trigger): (1) Aya Nomura individual stake invisibly trims (Keikyu vehicle-hopping pattern - monitoring opacity higher than other Murakami names since Aya Nomura can trim 3.97% by up to 1pp without triggering refile), (2) Px breaks above ¥1,400 = activist alpha extracted. DATA: May 13 earnings is the next hard catalyst window.
watch_list.notes: MIRROR IMAGE OF EXEDY: there it was Murakami stage 5 exit (cut); here it's stage 1-2 fresh entry. Aggregate stake structure: Aya Nomura 3.97%, ATRA 0.98%, C&I residual = ~5%. ROE cap at 4.9% means Murakami can re-rate to 0.85x PBR but no further. Recommend: WAIT for May 13 earnings entry window or for ATRA stake escalation signal. If WAIT chosen, refresh PWER on next change report.

watch_list.name: Daikin Industries
watch_list.status: ELLIOTT MODE 1 FRESH - ~3% stake disclosed Apr 16 + presentation Apr 26 (sub-5% EDINET)
watch_list.thesis: Elliott Mode 1 fresh campaign on global HVAC leader (¥6T mkt cap). Stake ~3% (sub-5% EDINET). Apr 16 disclosure pop +13.9%. Apr 26 public presentation released. Demands: margin improvement (lag Carrier/Trane/Lennox global peers), buyback, non-core review (chemicals 10%), deeper business integration, sharper strategy. Q3 op profit -15% YoY missed estimates -40% gives Elliott credibility on margin pressure.
watch_list.trigger: PRIMARY (BUY trigger): (1) Elliott crosses 5% threshold = 大量保有 EDINET disclosure (conviction confirmation), (2) Daikin announces buyback or non-core review in response, (3) May 12 earnings creates pullback to ¥19,500-20,500 entry zone (Δ vs WAC +5-10%). BEAR signal: Elliott exits before forcing change; HVAC cycle deteriorates further. DATA: Verify estimated WAC ¥18,500 with Bloomberg pre-Apr-16 trading data.
watch_list.notes: Px ¥21,475 = +16.1% above Elliott est. WAC ¥18,500 = INSIDE +25% rule but PAST +15% co-investment edge. PWER 11.5% sub-threshold = WAIT for either pullback or stake escalation. Daikin structurally HARDER target than MOL (large cap, founder legacy, quality compounder reputation, operating issues partly cyclical not just governance). Morningstar DCF FV ¥57,775 implies long-term structural upside but activist horizon shorter.
